description: FMFX fork rebase — Skoruba 3.0.0-rc1 baseline (Story 1). Re-baseline the fork onto upstream release/3.0.0-rc1 with no FMFX customisations applied; later stories port bespoke FMFX code.

userStories.title: Verify clean upstream build on .NET 10 SDK
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a documented, reproducible green build of upstream release/3.0.0-rc1 from the new working tree so we have a known-good baseline before applying any FMFX changes.
acceptanceCriteria: `dotnet --version` reports a `10.0.x` SDK; if a `global.json` exists at repo root it pins a .NET 10 SDK version, otherwise the SDK version used is documented in `README.md` setup instructions
`npm ci` succeeds in `src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.UI.Client/`
`npm run build:spa` succeeds in `src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.UI.Client/` and produces output under `src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.UI.Spa/wwwroot/`
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds with zero errors
`dotnet test tests/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.UnitTests` passes
`dotnet test tests/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.IntegrationTests` passes (matches upstream rc1 green state)
`dotnet test tests/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.STS.IntegrationTests` passes (matches upstream rc1 green state)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-05-11: .NET 10.0.203 SDK, global.json created at repo root, dotnet build succeeded (0 errors, 0 warnings), npm restore + build:spa both green, all tests pass (174 unit + 39 API unit + 78 API integration + 23 STS integration = 314 total). Solution at repo root, not src/.

userStories.title: Bump fork version to 3.0.0-FMFX.1
userStories.description: As a downstream consumer, I want fork-published packages to carry a 3.0.0-FMFX.1 version so I can identify them as Freemarket's rebase-baseline build, distinct from upstream 3.0.0-preview.N and from the legacy 2.3.0-FMFX.N line.
acceptanceCriteria: `Directory.Build.props` `<Version>` element is set to `3.0.0-FMFX.1`

No other version-bearing files diverge from upstream `release/3.0.0-rc1` in this story (verify via `git diff upstream/release/3.0.0-rc1 -- '**/*.csproj' '**/Directory.Build.props' '**/*.props' '**/package.json'`)
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds with no new warnings beyond upstream rc1 baseline
All `tests/*` test projects pass (`dotnet test` on UnitTests, IntegrationTests, STS.IntegrationTests)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-05-11: Directory.Build.props <Version> set to 3.0.0-FMFX.1. Build succeeded (0 errors, 0 warnings). All 314 tests pass (174 unit + 39 API unit + 78 API integration + 23 STS integration).

userStories.title: Update publish workflow to target FreemarketFX GitHub Packages
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want the NuGet publish workflow on the new branch to push to the FreemarketFX GitHub Packages feed (not upstream's destination), so subsequent stories can produce consumable 3.0.0-FMFX.N packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish workflow YAML in `.github/workflows/` runs `dotnet pack` and `dotnet nuget push` targeting `https://nuget.pkg.github.com/FreemarketFX/index.json`
Authentication uses the FreemarketFX GitHub Packages publish secret name mirroring the previous fork's publish workflow on old `main`
Workflow triggers include push/release for `feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1` (and `main` once promoted)
Workflow does NOT push from upstream-tracking branches (no `release/3.0.0-rc1` or `release/*` triggers on the publish job)
Workflow YAML is syntactically valid (verified via `actionlint` or equivalent, or visual inspection if no linter available)
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds
All `tests/*` test projects pass
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-05-11: Created .github/workflows/nuget_publish.yml targeting https://nuget.pkg.github.com/FreemarketFX/index.json using ADMIN_PUBLISH_NUGET_TOKEN (mirroring old main workflow). Triggers on push to main/feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1, release published, and workflow_dispatch. No release/* upstream branches in triggers. Uses dotnet pack on solution + glob push with --skip-duplicate.

userStories.title: Re-enable CodeQL workflow on the new branch
userStories.description: As a security owner, I want CodeQL scanning enabled on the new branch from day one so security findings track from the baseline, not from when bespoke code lands.
acceptanceCriteria: A CodeQL workflow file exists in `.github/workflows/` (ported from the previous fork's `chore(FMFX-8682): Setup GitHub Advanced Security` commit if not already present from upstream)
Workflow triggers on push and pull_request targeting `feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1`
Languages list includes both `csharp` and `javascript-typescript` (covers the React SPA)
CodeQL config file (if used) has no broken globs; YAML is syntactically valid (visual inspection or actionlint)
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds
All `tests/*` test projects pass
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-05-11: Created .github/workflows/codeql.yml with push/pull_request triggers on main and feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1, matrix scanning csharp and javascript-typescript, explicit .NET 10 setup + build step for csharp language. No CodeQL config file used (no broken globs). YAML-only change; build/tests unchanged from US-003.

userStories.title: Update README with new baseline and re-base strategy
userStories.description: As anyone landing on the repo, I want the README to make clear that this is now a fork of upstream release/3.0.0-rc1, that the long-lived branch has changed, and what the re-base plan is, so I don't act on stale assumptions from the old 1.2.0-based code.
acceptanceCriteria: `README.md` has a 'Freemarket fork' section at the top stating: upstream baseline is `skoruba/Duende.IdentityServer.Admin@release/3.0.0-rc1`
README states the active branch is `feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1` (will become `main` after the rebase epic completes)
README states the package versioning convention `3.0.0-FMFX.N`
README states the re-base plan: re-base onto stable `3.0.0` once Skoruba tags it
README links to `CLAUDE.md` in this repo and to `Freemarket.Identity/CLEANUP-PLAN.md` for the epic-level plan
No upstream README content deleted beyond what's needed to avoid direct contradiction with the Freemarket fork section
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds
All `tests/*` test projects pass
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Verified 2026-05-11: Added '## Freemarket Fork' section to README.md immediately after the badges, before the Versions section. Section states upstream baseline, active branch, package versioning (3.0.0-FMFX.N), re-base plan, and links to CLAUDE.md and Freemarket.Identity/CLEANUP-PLAN.md. No upstream content removed. Build/tests unchanged from US-004 (README-only change, no source files modified).

userStories.title: Prepare branch for PR (do not push, do not merge)
userStories.description: As the developer running this story, I want the branch in a clean, reviewable state with all commits clearly attributable, so the human reviewer can verify each step before pushing or opening a PR.
acceptanceCriteria: `git status` shows a clean working tree on `feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1`
`git log feature/rebase-onto-3.0.0-rc1 ^upstream/release/3.0.0-rc1 --oneline` shows only commits from US-001 through US-005 plus the setup commits (claude-shared subtree add + CLAUDE.md + PRD)
Each commit message in that range follows Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, etc.) and ends with the standard `Co-Authored-By: Claude (<model-id>) <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer
No `git push` to origin has been executed; no PR has been opened
`dotnet build src/Skoruba.Duende.IdentityServer.Admin.sln --configuration Release` succeeds
All `tests/*` test projects pass
Typecheck passes (dotnet build with no errors)
Tests pass
